title: Velmoc
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}}
{{Youmay|the sentient species known as the Velmoc|[[Velmor/Legends|Velmor]], their homeworld}}

{{Quote|Despite what the Imperials say, I know the Velmoc as a proud, intelligent race of warriors.|Ergric Betos|<ref name="VRAR" />}}
The '''Velmoc''' [[Species/Legends|species]] were [[Species/Legends|sentient]], [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoids]] indigenous to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Velmor/Legends|Velmor]]. Their distinctive physiology included powerful [[Limb/Legends|limbs]], compound [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], two antennae, and prominent crests that jutted from the arms. The species inhabited the [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] of their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], sorting themselves into tribes. They were renowned for their [[Soldier/Legends|warrior]] culture by some of the [[Human/Legends|Humans]] who colonized Velmor; other Humans reviled them as primitives. Despite the sometime animosity between the two species, some Velmoc integrated into Human [[Velmorian]] society and worked as servants. The insectoids spoke [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] as a primary [[Language/Legends|language]].

By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the Velmoc were nearing [[Death/Legends|extinction]] on Velmor. After a [[Great Revolt|coup d'état]] destabilized the Velmorian civilization and ushered in a period of puppet rule under the aegis of the Empire, the Velmoc faced their potential [[Speciecide|extermination]] by the Imperial [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Zeta Traal]]. During an uprising that upended the Velmorian [[King/Legends|monarchy]], the crown [[Prince/Legends|prince]], [[Denid]], escaped the planet with the help of his Velmoc [[Aide/Legends|aide]], [[Jedidiah]], a one-time [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] hopeful who had turned down an offer of [[Jedi training/Legends|Jedi training]] to serve the royal family. With the help of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[operative]]s, Denid and Jedidiah returned to Velmor, a homecoming that eventually prompted a confrontation between Jedidiah and Traal. The encounter resulted in Jedidiah's death, but it also removed Traal from her position of influence, thus freeing Denid to become the king of the Velmorians.

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|Not even medical droids can work miracles… The Human [''sic''] brain cannot be replaced bionically… and his mind is damaged beyond simple repair!|A [[2-1B surgical droid/Legends|2-1B medical droid]], regarding the brain-damaged Velmoc patient Jedidiah|<ref name="SW49" />}}
Velmoc were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Insect/Legends|insectoids]]<ref name="VRAR" /> whose bodies followed a more-or-less [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] structure. Each of a Velmoc's muscular [[Limb/Legends|legs]] terminated in three long, flat toes, while each hand sported four fingers with sharp claws. A flat, wedge-shaped crest adorned each arm from the upper portion where it attached to the elbow, across the elbow itself, and to the top of the forearm. Members of the [[Species/Legends|species]] could be strong, hardy beings, with a height exceeding that of an average [[Human/Legends|Human]]. The insectoids' bodies were [[Color/Legends|colored]] green or dull purple.<ref name="SW49" />

The Velmoc [[Skeleton/Legends|skull]] housed the [[Brain/Legends|brain]],<ref name="SW49" /> an organ otherwise vulnerable to injury such that a blow to the head might result in brain damage and cause the victim to devolve into gibbering insanity.<ref name="VRAR" /> Two compound [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], colored gray or red, protruded from the sides of the head. A large maw gaped from the front of the face; the lower, cantilevered jaw was shovel-like, with upward-pointing [[Tooth/Legends|tooth]]-like projections that fit into slots in the upper mandible. A crest extended from the back of the head and gave way to a rectangular patch of striated skin at the back of the neck.[[Hair/Legends|Hairlike]] growths sprouted from above the eyes, the sides of the face, and the chin. Two antennae extended from the top of the head.<ref name="SW49" />

Some Velmoc showed signs of [[Force-sensitive/Legends|sensitivity]] to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Humans were susceptible to a malady known as the [[Velmoc flu]], although they gained immunity to the illness if they survived a bout of it.<ref name="VRAR" />

==Society and culture==
{{Quote|Some Velmoc have wormed their way into proper Velmorian society, mostly as servants or freak-show entertainments. (The most highly trained domestic Velmoc in history was probably the royal aide who spirited Prince Denid to his doom.)|Captain Zeta Traal|<ref name="VRAR" />}}
As a tribal species, the Velmoc lived in small groups in the [[Mountain/Legends|mountains]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Velmor/Legends|Velmor]]. They were self-respecting and canny [[Soldier/Legends|warriors]],<ref name="VRAR" /> capable of employing their flexible fingers to use tools and wield weapons, such as long [[Staff/Legends|staffs]]. At least one member of the species wore clothing, which consisted of a segmented tunic, a belt, and [[Boot/Legends|boots]] fitted to the Velmoc anatomy. Some Velmoc learned to ride a native [[Velmorian]] beast known as the [[ycaqt]].<ref name="SW49" /> At least one Velmoc tribe, the [[Velmoc-Dac]], accepted a Human into their ranks: [[Ergric Betos]]. Nevertheless, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Zeta Traal]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] disdained the entire species, with particular rancor reserved for the mountain-dwelling segments, whom she characterized as primitive.<ref name="VRAR" />

Velmoc spoke [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] as their primary [[Language/Legends|language]]. Velmoc names might be shortened for the convenience of members of other species; for instance, the name Jedidiah became Jedi. Indeed, some members of the species integrated into the society of the Human Velmorians, with whom the Velmoc shared their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. Such Velmoc often found employment as retainers, although others took on the role of [[Entertainer|sideshow entertainments]].<ref name="VRAR" /> Despite the prejudice shown to the Velmoc by the Human Velmorians, members of the two species could become devoted and faithful friends.<ref name="SW49" />

==History==
[[File:JedidiahEscape.jpg|thumb|right|upright|The Velmoc retainer Jedidiah helped Prince Denid escape Velmor.]]
{{Quote|Though nearly extinct, the Velmoc could prove to be a variable in the Velmor equation. This primitive insectoid trash was already dying out when settlers arrived, but a few tribes have clung on tenaciously in the Tol Velmoc mountains.|Captain Zeta Traal|<ref name="VRAR" />}}
The Velmoc evolved on the planet Velmor, a world of [[Tree/Legends|forests]] and [[Grass/Legends|grasslands]] with a [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]] of 0.92 standard. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] Human colonists from the [[Empress Teta system/Legends|Empress Teta system]] reached Velmor, millennia before the outbreak of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Velmoc population was already in decline. The planet and its parent [[Star system/Legends|system]], [[Velm system|Velm]], became part of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] and [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. Nevertheless, the planet was never highly integrated into [[The galaxy/Legends|the wider galaxy]].<ref name="VRAR" />

While the Velmoc maintained their tribal existence in the world's mountains, the Human colonists established a [[King/Legends|monarchy]] and founded [[City/Legends|settlements]], such as [[Den Velmor]], which became the planetary [[Capital/Legends|capital]]. The population of the planet's native sentients continued to decline, however, and by the time of the Galactic Civil War, the species was nearing extinction; they comprised a mere 0.3 percent of the planet's population of 892,000. Those tribes that did eke out a traditional livelihood occupied the [[Tol Velmoc mountains|Tol Velmoc Mountains]].<ref name="VRAR" />

In the [[Velmorian calendar|313th year]] of the [[Mrid]]—approximately [[20 BBY/Legends|twenty years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]—the Human King [[Lorac]] and his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]], [[Denira]], were ousted from power in an Imperial-supported [[Great Revolt|uprising]]. A Velmoc named [[Jedidiah]], who served as an [[Aide/Legends|aide]] to the royal family<ref name="VRAR" /> and went by the nickname Jedi,<ref name="SW49" /> whisked away the fifteen-year-old crown [[Prince/Legends|prince]], [[Denid]], and his betrothed, [[Loren (noble)|Loren]]. They fled to the nearby [[Tantajo system/Legends|Tantajo system]], but their poorly equipped [[Starship/Legends|starship]] failed, and they crashed on the planet [[Tantajoc V]].<ref name="VRAR" /> Loren did not survive the crash.<ref name="SW49" /> Despite insanity born of a head injury<ref name="VRAR" /> and extreme isolation,<ref name="CSWE II 148">{{CSWECite|II|148|Jedidiah}}</ref> Jedidiah remained with Denid and helped his Human charge thrive on the forbidding planet for twenty years.<ref name="VRAR" />

[[File:JedidiahDebris.jpg|thumb|left|Injury sustained after escaping Velmor rendered the Velmoc Jedidiah a gibbering idiot.]]
Meanwhile, the [[Regent/Legends|regent]], [[Zelor]], took over rule of Velmor. He came under the influence of an Imperial captain, a Human woman named Zeta Traal. Traal orchestrated a plan whereby a few [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], the prince [[Anod]] would throw the planet's allegiances behind the Galactic Empire—at Zelor's urging—upon his official coronation, an event that could only happen twenty years after Prince Denid's disappearance, as prescribed by Velmorian law. Meanwhile, Traal recommended that the Empire [[Orbital bombardment/Legends|bombard]] the Tel Velmoc mountains from [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] to exterminate the Velmoc and terrify the Human Velmorians. When Ergric Betos, one of Anod's [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguards]], was [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] from Velmorian society, the Velmoc-Dac tribe accepted him into their ranks as a "soft-skin warrior." His time with the insectoids granted him a deep respect for their culture.<ref name="VRAR" />

Nevertheless, Jedidiah and Denid's [[Distress beacon/Legends|distress signal]] was finally detected; a band of [[operative]]s from the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]—the Humans [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and the [[Droid/Legends|droids]] [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]—followed the signal to Tantajoc V and rescued Denid and his Velmoc protector. With Skywalker disguised as a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] named [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Korl Marcus]] and Organa as the prince's late fiancée, Loren,<ref name="SW49" /> the group ventured back to Velmor to stop the coronation of Anod. Moments before Anod could become king, the outsiders appeared. When [[Blood/Legends|blood]] tests proved Denid was who he claimed to be, Traal called a royal forest [[Hunting/Legends|hunt]]—actually part of a plot to kill the returning prince. Her plan went awry, however, when Jedidiah appeared riding a ycaqt. He attacked Traal to protect the others, but he was fatally shot in the melee. Anod, too, was killed in the forest. Jedidiah's death thus paved the way for the coronation of Denid, which occurred on [[Arloc]] 24th, the 310th Year of the Ycaqt. Years later, Velmor became a member world of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="VRAR" />

==Velmoc in the galaxy==
{{Quote|The Velmoc, Jedidiah, supposedly had once been offered training as a Jedi, but had refused, sensing the royal family was in trouble. According to Denid, Jedidiah sustained head injuries while in exile that left him little more than a rambling lunatic…. The news that Jedidiah had been reduced to this state was disheartening indeed.|Ergric Betos|<ref name="VRAR" />}}
The Velmoc Jedidiah had occasion to venture from Velmor on at least two occasions. On the first, the Velmoc was determined to be Force-sensitive. The [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] offered to train the Velmoc as a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], an aspiration Jedidiah deeply desired. Nevertheless, he felt that the royal family needed his help and would even come to ruin if he left for [[Jedi training/Legends|Jedi training]]. Accordingly, Jedidiah declined the Jedi's invitation and remained on Velmor. Nevertheless, Jedidiah left Velmor for two decades after the Imperial-sparked coup overthrew the king. He subsisted on the planet Tantajoc V with Prince Denid until returning to Velmor with the aid of Rebel Alliance agents.<ref name="VRAR" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|It was pretty awful and depressing when it came out.|Walt Simonson, on the print job of ''Star Wars 49: The Last Jedi''|<ref name="HTDSWTMW 8">{{StoryCite|story=How to Do Star Wars the Marvel Way|book=Wikipedia:Back Issue!|bformatted=''Back Issue!'' 9}}</ref>}}
The Velmoc were created for [[Star Wars (1977) 49|''Star Wars'' (1977) 49]], an issue of the [[Marvel Comics]] [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series that was first published in [[July]] [[1981]]. The issue was written by [[Mike W. Barr]] and illustrated by [[Walter Simonson|Walt Simonson]] and [[Tom Palmer]]. Barr's idea for the alien Jedidiah sprang from his desire to create a foil for Luke Skywalker; in contrast to the fledgling Force-user Skywalker, who wants nothing more than to be Jedi, Jedidiah is someone who had the opportunity to become a Jedi but turned it down.<ref name="HTDSWTMW 8" /> Discrepancies in the coloring by [[Don Warfield]] and [[Christie Scheele]] depict the Velmoc character Jedidiah as green with red eyes on the cover, but dull purple with gray eyes in the issue itself. The information above assumes that both are valid phenotypes for the species. Another discrepancy arises when a [[Unidentified 2-1B surgical droid (Jedidiah)|medical droid]] suggests that Jedidiah has a "Human brain," despite his obvious non-Human nature.<ref name="SW49" /> The issue had problems with printing as well; Simonson later recalled that he was aghast at the poor quality of the final printed issue. [[Dark Horse Comics]] recolored and reprinted the issue in [[November]] [[2002]] as part of ''[[Star Wars: A Long Time Ago... Volume 3: Resurrection of Evil]]''; the re-issue shows the Velmoc Jedidiah and the other characters as they were originally intended to be depicted.<ref name="HTDSWTMW 8" />

Author [[Cory Herndon]] added new information on the Velmoc and their relationship with the Velmorians in his article "[[Velmor: Royalty and Rebellion]]," published in [[March]] [[2003]] by [[Wizards of the Coast]]. The article is part of the ''[[Planet Hoppers]]'' series that describe planets in terms usable for the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]''.
